title: Angkor Thom - Wikipedia
highlights: "Great City"), alternatively Nokor Thom  ...    state temple, the Bayon, with the other major sites clustered around the Victory Square immediately to the  ...    pronunciation. The word Nokor (Khmer: នគរ, Nôkô) is literally derived from Sanskrit word of Nagara (Devanāgarī: नगर), which means City, combining with Khmer word Thom (Khmer: ធំ, Thum), which means Big or Great so as to form Nokor Thom then being altered to current name of Angkor Thom.  ...    Angkor Thom is in the Bayon style. This manifests itself in the large scale of the construction, in the widespread use of laterite, in the face-towers at each of the entrances to the city and in the naga-carrying  ...    The city lies on the west bank of the Siem Reap River, a tributary of Tonle Sap, about 0.25 mi (0.40 km) from the river. The south gate of Angkor Thom is 7.2 km (4.5 mi) north of Siem Reap and 1.7 km (1.1 mi) north of the entrance to Angkor Wat. The walls, 8 m (26 ft) high and flanked by a moat, are each 3 km (1.9 mi)
